items.title: Marked-up Mac minis flood eBay amid shortages driven by AI
items.description: Apple’s sold-out Mac mini is spawning marked-up eBay listings as demand surges for the compact desktop, now favored for running local AI models and tools.

items.title: Nuclear startup X-energy raises $1B in data center-driven IPO
items.description: Amazon-backed X-energy was able to raise around 20% more money in its IPO as demand for nuclear power surges.

items.title: Another spyware maker caught distributing fake Android snooping apps
items.description: Researchers have found a new case where government authorities used a fake Android app to plant spyware on a target’s phone. The company that allegedly developed the spyware was not previously known to sell this type of software.

items.description: Nothing's new on-device dictation tool supports over 100 languages.

items.title: DeepSeek previews new AI model that ‘closes the gap’ with frontier models
items.description: DeepSeek says both models are more efficient and performant than DeepSeek V3.2 due to architectural improvements, and have almost "closed the gap" with current leading models, both open and closed, on reasoning benchmarks.

items.title: In another wild turn for AI chips, Meta signs deal for millions of Amazon AI CPUs
items.description: Meta has commandeered a big chunk of Amazon's homegrown CPUs (not GPUs) for AI agentic workloads, signaling that a new kind of chip race has begun.

items.title: Porsche is adding an all-electric Cayenne coupe to its lineup
items.description: Porsche will start selling the Cayenne Coupe Electric in late summer.

items.title: Bob Iger rejoins Thrive Capital as advisor after Disney exit
items.description: The former Disney CEO hold a stake in Thrive and has previously held the role of the venture partner at the firm.

items.description: The soldier is accused of using classified government information to inform a wager on the prediction market Polymarket.

items.title: Redwood Materials loses COO amid layoffs, restructuring
items.description: Former Tesla executive Chris Lister is retiring, and at least three other VPs have left Redwood around the recent restructuring, TechCrunch has learned.

items.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
items.description: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

items.title: Bret Taylor’s Sierra buys YC-backed AI startup Fragment
items.description: Sierra, the AI customer service agent startup founded by technologist Bret Taylor, announced today that it has acquired the YC-backed French startup Fragment.

items.title: Instagram tests a new ‘Instants’ app for sharing disappearing photos
items.description: The app lets users share disappearing photos with their friends that can be viewed only once and remain available for 24 hours.

items.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
items.description: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

items.title: Trump’s pick to run US cyber agency CISA asks to drop out
items.description: Sean Plankey has requested to withdraw his name to run the U.S. cybersecurity agency after a tumultuous year of chaotic temporary leadership.

items.title: Don’t stop hiring humans — stop hiring the wrong humans, Artisan’s founder says
items.description: Artisan may be known for their bold “Stop Hiring Humans” campaign but the reality is every founder needs to assemble the right team if they want to scale.
